Reasoning
Q1
Directions:Read the information carefully and
answer the questions given below.
 
At a reunion party, eight friends named P, Q, R, S,
T, U, V and W sit together on a round table facing
the center. Interestingly, all eight friends are
pursuing bachelor’s degrees in different subjects
namely Mathematics, English, Business studies,
Polity, Physics, Chemistry, Mythology and Hindi.
They are not necessarily seated in the mentioned
order. R sits adjacent to S who is studying
Business studies. Q is studying Polity and does
not sit adjacent to R or S. U is sitting fifth to the
left of P and is studying Physics. One who is
studying Chemistry sits immediate left to U. V
sits opposite to P. Neither W nor T is immediate
neighbor of V. One who is studying Hindi sits
fifth to the left of V, who is learning Mythology. T
is studying English and sits next to the one who is
studying Hindi.
 
What does the person study who is sitting third
to the left of R?
(A) Mathematics
(B) Physics
(C) Mythology
(D) Polity
(E) Hindi

answer the questions given below.

studying Chemistry sits immediate left to U. V
sits opposite to P. Neither W nor T is immediate
neighbor of V. One who is studying Hindi sits
fifth to the left of V, who is learning Mythology. T
is studying English and sits next to the one who is
studying Hindi.
 
Which subject is P studying?
(A) Mythology
(B) English
(C) Chemistry
(D) Polity
(E) Mathematics

answer the questions given below.

Level-1

Who is diagonally opposite to Q?

Q6
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below:
 
Seven persons i.e. Z, Y, X, W, V, U and T of
different ages i.e. 6, 15, 19, 23, 30, 45, 60 years
are sitting in a circular table facing towards the
center. Note- No two consecutive letters are in
alphabetical order. Person’s age must not be a
factor or a multiple of the sum of the ages of
their immediate neighbors. X sits immediate to
the right of the person whose age is 15. One
person sits between V and X . One person sits
between Z and the person whose age is 6 . Age
of V is twice the age of the person who sits 2nd
to the right of V. Difference between the age of
the immediate neighbors of T is more than 14.
Age of U must not be multiple of 10. Sum of the
ages of immediate neighbors of X is 34. Y is
younger than Z.
 
What is the age of Z?
(A) 15
(B) 60
(C) 23
(D) 6
(E) 19
Q7
Study the following information carefully and
answer the questions given below:
 
Seven persons i.e. Z, Y, X, W, V, U and T of
different ages i.e. 6, 15, 19, 23, 30, 45, 60 years
are sitting in a circular table facing towards the

center. Note- No two consecutive letters are in
alphabetical order. Person’s age must not be a
factor or a multiple of the sum of the ages of
their immediate neighbors. X sits immediate to
the right of the person whose age is 15. One
person sits between V and X . One person sits
between Z and the person whose age is 6 . Age
of V is twice the age of the person who sits 2nd
to the right of V. Difference between the age of
the immediate neighbors of T is more than 14.
Age of U must not be multiple of 10. Sum of the
ages of immediate neighbors of X is 34. Y is
younger than Z.
 
What is the position of W with respect to X?
(A) Immediate left
(B) 4th to the right
(C) 2nd to the left
(D) Immediate left
(E) None of these

Q11
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance
between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing inside the circle. G and
A are immediate neighbors of each other. There
are three people sitting between A and F. C sits
second to the left of F. The number of people
sitting between G and C is the same number of
people sitting between C and E. B sits second to
the right of D, who does not sit near to F.
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way and so form a group. Which one does not
belong to that group?
(A) E-H
(B) A-D
(C) B-F
(D) G-A
(E) H-F
Q12
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance
between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing inside the circle. G and
A are immediate neighbors of each other. There
are three people sitting between A and F. C sits
second to the left of F. The number of people
sitting between G and C is the same number of
people sitting between C and E. B sits second to
the right of D, who does not sit near to F.
How many people are sitting between B and E?
(A) One
(B) Three
(C) Two
(D) Four
(E) None of these

counted from right of D ?

(B) Five
(C) Three
(D) Two
(E) None of these

Q16
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance
between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing inside the circle. V sits
third to the left of W. There is only one person
sitting between V and Q. R sits third to the right
of Q. S and R sit adjacent to each other. S does
not sits opposite to V. P is the immediate
neighbor of T. There are two people sitting
between P and U.
As many people sitting between R and T as many
between W and _ ?
(A) V
(B) S
(C) Q
(D) P
(E) U
Q17

neighbor of T. There are two people sitting
between P and U.

(A) One
(B) Two
(C) Four
(D) Five
(E) Either Two or Four

(E) W
Q20
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people Z, Y, X, W, V, U, T and S are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance

between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing outside the circle. W
sits three places away from the T. X sits second to
the left of W. There are two people sitting
between X and Z. Z sits opposite to U. There are
two people sitting between Y and V. Both V and
Y does not sit near to X.
How many people are sitting between W and T?
(A) One
(B) Three
(C) None
(D) Four
(E) Five
Q21
Directions: Study the following data carefully

Y does not sit near to X.
If V sits opposite to T, then who sits third to the
left of U ?
(A) Y
(B) W
(C) U
(D) X
(E) S
Q22
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.

between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing outside the circle. W
sits three places away from the T. X sits second to

two people sitting between Y and V. Both V and
Y does not sit near to X.
What is the position of V with respect to S?
(A) Immediate left
(B) Third left
(C) Second right
(D) Either (A) or (C) option
(E) None of these
Q23
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people Z, Y, X, W, V, U, T and S are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance
between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing outside the circle. W
sits three places away from the T. X sits second to

between X and Z. Z sits opposite to U. There are
two people sitting between Y and V. Both V and
Y does not sit near to X.
Which of the following statements is true?
(A) V sits just right of Z
(B) Two people sit between X and W
(C) S sits opposite to X
(D) Y sits second to the right of U
(E) None of these
Q24
Directions: Study the following data carefully
and answer the questions accordingly.
Eight people Z, Y, X, W, V, U, T and S are sitting
around a circular table with equal distance
between each other but not necessarily in the
same order. All are facing outside the circle. W
sits three places away from the T. X sits second to
the left of W. There are two people sitting
between X and Z. Z sits opposite to U. There are
two people sitting between Y and V. Both V and
Y does not sit near to X.
Who sits third to the right of Z ?
(A) W
(B) V
(C) Y
(D) X
(E) S

Hints & Solutions
Q1
Text Solution:
1. U is sitting fifth to the left of P and is studying
Physics.
2. V sits opposite to P.
3. One who is studying Chemistry sits immediate
left to U.
4. One who is studying Hindi sits fifth to the left
of V, who is learning Mythology.
5. T is studying English and sits next to the one
who is studying Hindi.
6. R sits adjacent to S who is studying Business
studies.
7. Q is studying Polity and does not sit adjacent
to R or S. 
8. Neither W nor T is immediate neighbor of V. 
Thus, V is sitting third to the left of R and studies
Mythology. Hence, the correct option is (C).
Q2
Text Solution:
1. U is sitting fifth to the left of P and is studying
Physics.
2. V sits opposite to P.
3. One who is studying Chemistry sits immediate
left to U.
4. One who is studying Hindi sits fifth to the left
of V, who is learning Mythology.
5. T is studying English and sits next to the one
who is studying Hindi.
6. R sits adjacent to S who is studying Business
studies.
7. Q is studying Polity and does not sit adjacent
to R or S. 
8. Neither W nor T is immediate neighbor of V. 
Thus, P is studying Mathematics.
Hence, the correct option is (E).
Q3
Text Solution:
1. U is sitting fifth to the left of P and is studying
Physics.
2. V sits opposite to P.
3. One who is studying Chemistry sits immediate
left to U.
4. One who is studying Hindi sits fifth to the left
of V, who is learning Mythology.
5. T is studying English and sits next to the one
who is studying Hindi.
6. R sits adjacent to S who is studying Business
studies.
7. Q is studying Polity and does not sit adjacent
to R or S. 
8. Neither W nor T is immediate neighbor of V. 
Thus, S is sitting diagonally opposite to Q.
Hence, the correct option is A
Q4
Text Solution:
1. U is sitting fifth to the left of P and is studying
Physics.
2. V sits opposite to P.

3. One who is studying Chemistry sits immediate
left to U.
4. One who is studying Hindi sits fifth to the left
of V, who is learning Mythology.
5. T is studying English and sits next to the one
who is studying Hindi.
6. R sits adjacent to S who is studying Business
studies.
7. Q is studying Polity and does not sit adjacent
to R or S. 
8. Neither W nor T is immediate neighbor of V. 
Thus, R is studying Chemistry. Hence, the correct
option is (D).
Q5
Text Solution:
1. U is sitting fifth to the left of P and is studying
Physics.
2. V sits opposite to P.
3. One who is studying Chemistry sits immediate
left to U.
4. One who is studying Hindi sits fifth to the left
of V, who is learning Mythology.
5. T is studying English and sits next to the one
who is studying Hindi.
6. R sits adjacent to S who is studying Business
studies.
7. Q is studying Polity and does not sit adjacent
to R or S. 
8. Neither W nor T is immediate neighbor of V. 
Thus, W sits between P and T. Hence, the correct
option is (B).
Q6
Text Solution:
X sits immediate to the right of the person whose
age is 15.
One person sits between V and X.
Sum of the ages of immediate neighbors of X is
34.

Age of V is twice the age of the person who sits
2nd to the right of V.
(Remaining ages are 6, 23, 30, 45 and 60, so use
30 and 60. Person’s age must not be a factor or a
multiple of the sum of the ages of their
immediate neighbors.)
Case 1 is eliminated because 15 is the factor or a
multiple of the sum of the ages of their
immediate neighbors.
One person sits between Z and the person
whose age is 6.(Person’s age must not be a factor
or a multiple of the sum of the ages of their
immediate neighbors.)
No two consecutive letters are in alphabetical
order.(Now Y is fixed.)
Age of U must not be multiple of 10.
(No two consecutive letters are in alphabetical
order. U is not sit near to V)
Fixed remaining person uses of No two
consecutive letters are in alphabetical order.(W is
not sit near to X)
Person’s age must not be a factor or a multiple of
the sum of the ages of their immediate
neighbors. 45 is not fixed in case 3 so it is
eliminated.
Difference between the age of the immediate
neighbors of T is more than 14.
Y is younger than Z.

Q11

G and A are immediate neighbors of each other.
There are three people sitting between A and F.
C sits second to the left of F.
The number of people sitting between G and C is
the same number of people sitting between C
and E.
B sits second to the right of D, who does not sit
near to F.
Case 2: is eliminated.
